questions.text: Who is the author of 'Silk Road'?
questions.explanation: Nick Middleton is an adventurer and physical geographer who wrote this travelogue.

questions.text: What was the destination of the author's journey?
questions.explanation: The author was traveling to Mount Kailash to perform the 'Kora' (circumambulation).

questions.text: What is the 'Kora'?
options: A religious circumambulation of a sacred site
A type of Tibetan mountain goat
The name of the author's car
questions.explanation: Performing the Kora at Mount Kailash is a significant spiritual act in Buddhism and Hinduism.

questions.text: Who was Tsetan?
questions.explanation: Tsetan was hired to drive the author from Ravu to the sacred mountain.

questions.text: What gift did Lhamo give the author before he left Ravu?
questions.explanation: She gave him the coat to protect him from the extreme cold during his journey.

questions.text: Which animal did the author encounter on the high plains of Tibet?
questions.explanation: The author encountered various wild animals, including dkyang and fierce mastiffs protecting nomad tents.

questions.text: What were the Tibetan Mastiffs used for by the nomads?
questions.explanation: They are huge, aggressive black dogs used to protect the nomads' tents (droba).

questions.text: The author described the town of Hor as:
options: A grim, miserable place with no vegetation and much refuse
questions.explanation: Hor was a disappointment to the author, being dusty and neglected despite its proximity to Lake Mansarovar.

questions.text: Lake Mansarovar is considered sacred by:
questions.explanation: The lake is an ancient religious site for multiple religions, including Jainism and Bon.

questions.text: What problem did the author face in Darchen?
options: He had a severe cold and breathing trouble due to altitude
questions.explanation: The thin air and cold led to a blocked nostril and chest congestion, preventing him from sleeping.

questions.text: Tsetan took the author to a ________ in Darchen.
questions.explanation: The Tibetan doctor provided traditional herbal medicine that helped the author recover.

questions.text: Why was the author worried about being alone in Darchen?
options: There were no other pilgrims to accompany him for the Kora
questions.explanation: He had arrived early in the season, and the town was almost empty of the pilgrims he had hoped to join.

questions.text: Who was Norbu?
options: A Tibetan working at the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences
questions.explanation: Norbu was also in Darchen to perform the Kora and became the author's companion.

questions.text: Why did Norbu want to do the Kora?
options: For academic research and religious reasons
questions.explanation: He was writing papers on the Kora and wanted to experience it himself.

questions.text: What was the main challenge during the drive at 5,210 meters?
questions.explanation: Tsetan had to drive carefully over frozen snow and around sharp bends to avoid sliding off the track.

questions.text: What did the author use to help him breathe better in Darchen?
options: Herbal medicine from the Tibetan doctor
questions.explanation: He was given a five-day course of brown powders that looked like cinnamon and worked wonders.

questions.text: Why did Tsetan leave the author in Darchen?
options: He had to return to Lhasa for work
questions.explanation: Tsetan's job was to deliver him there safely; once the author was better, Tsetan returned.

questions.text: Norbu was different from other pilgrims because:
options: He was fat and not very spiritual/practical in his approach
questions.explanation: Norbu admitted he was too fat to do the prostrations and lacked the typical religious fervor, which the author found relatable.

questions.text: The phrase 'Salt Flats' refers to:
options: Vast plains covered in salt deposits and brackish lakes
questions.explanation: The plateau was marked by salt-crusted areas from ancient oceans that once covered the region.

questions.text: The author's first stop after leaving Ravu was:
questions.explanation: They encountered nomads herding their flocks early in the journey.

questions.text: What did the author see at the top of the pass (5,515m)?
options: A cairn of rocks festooned with white silk scarves and prayer flags
questions.explanation: It is a traditional Tibetan way to mark high mountain passes.

questions.text: What was the author's occupation?
questions.explanation: The text implies he is an academic researcher or traveler.

questions.text: Darchen was located at the ________ of Mount Kailash.
questions.explanation: It is the starting point for the Kora around the mountain.

questions.text: The town of Hor is situated on the shore of:
questions.explanation: The author expected much from Hor due to its location by the famous sacred lake.

questions.text: What does the title 'Silk Road' refer to?
options: The ancient trade route connecting East and West through Central Asia
A path through a silk factory
questions.explanation: The author is traveling through parts of the historic Silk Road in Tibet.

questions.text: Which of the following was a challenge for the car during the journey?
options: The low atmospheric pressure caused the fuel to expand and hiss
questions.explanation: Tsetan had to unscrew the fuel cap to release the pressure building up in the gas tank.

questions.text: How did the author feel after taking the Tibetan medicine?
options: He slept soundly like a log
questions.explanation: The medicine cleared his congestion, and he finally got a full night's rest.

questions.text: The author found Norbu in a:
questions.explanation: They met at the only cafe in Darchen while Norbu was reading an English magazine.

questions.text: Tsetan was a ________ Buddhist.
questions.explanation: He believed that if the author died, it would be bad for business, but as a Buddhist, he knew the author's soul would be fine.

questions.text: The 'Dkyang' (Wild Asses) were seen:
questions.explanation: The author saw them in the uninhabited high-altitude plains.

questions.text: Who was Daniel?
options: A companion who traveled with the author to Hor
questions.explanation: Daniel accompanied the author part of the way before catching a truck back to Lhasa.

questions.text: The author's physical condition can be described as:
options: Struggling with the harsh climate and high altitude
questions.explanation: The high altitude and freezing cold took a significant toll on his health.

questions.text: What was the main emotion the author felt in Hor?
questions.explanation: He found the town dirty and lacking the spiritual aura he had read about in older travel books.

questions.text: Tibetan Mastiffs are also known as:
questions.explanation: They are famous for being fierce protectors of the Tibetan plateau.

questions.text: Mount Kailash is a source for which major rivers?
questions.explanation: It is geographically significant as the headwaters of these major Asian rivers.

questions.text: The car used for the journey was a:
questions.explanation: Tsetan used a sturdy four-wheel drive vehicle to navigate the rugged terrain.

questions.text: Why did the author think Norbu was an ideal companion?
options: He spoke English and shared a similar practical/secular outlook
He was very fast at walking
He had a lot of money
questions.explanation: Norbu's ability to communicate and his shared objective made the journey easier.

questions.text: The author's journey began in:
questions.explanation: He mentions the 'extended winter' and wanting to beat the crowds of the main season.

questions.text: What is the primary theme of the chapter?
options: The challenges and beauty of travel in extreme environments
questions.explanation: It focuses on the physical and logistical hurdles of an arduous journey to a sacred place.

questions.text: Did the author complete the Kora?
options: No, he went back from Darchen
The story ends before he starts
questions.explanation: The ending implies that he and Norbu set off together to perform the pilgrimage.
